         // Parsed as i64 - verify it's negative and won't fit in uint64\n            assert!(\n                field_value.is_i64(),\n                \"Field should be i64 ({})\",\n                description\n            );\n            let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n            assert!(int_value < 0, \"Field should be negative ({})\", description);\n\n            // Verify the actual value matches expected\n            assert_eq!(\n                int_value,\n                value_str.parse::<i64>().unwrap(),\n                \"Field value should match expected {} ({})\",\n                value_str,\n                description\n            );\n\n            // For uint64 conversion simulation - verify it would fail\n            // uint64 range is 0 to 18446744073709551615\n            let fits_in_uint64 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u64::MAX as i64;\n            assert!(\n                !fits_in_uint64,\n                \"Negative value {} should not fit in uint64 ({})\",\n                value_str, description\n            );\n\n            // Verify type mismatch error is properly created for uint64 context\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint64\", \"int64_negative\");\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for negative {} in uint64 context ({})\",\n                value_str,\n                description\n            );\n\n            let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n            assert!(\n                error_msg.contains(\"uint64\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n                \"Error should mention uint64/unsigned type for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n            assert!(\n                error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"int64\"),\n                \"Error should mention negative/int64 for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Floating Point Precision and Range Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_floating_point_overflow_values() {\n    /// Test: Floating point overflow scenarios\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding f64::MAX are properly handled.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | 1e400 | Value exceeding f64::MAX |\n    /// | 1e1000 | Extremely large value |\n    /// | 1.7976931348623157e+309 | f64::MAX + 1 |\n    /// | inf | Positive infinity |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"1e400\", \"exponential 400\"),\n        (\"1e1000\", \"exponential 1000\"),\n        (\"1.7976931348623157e+309\", \"f64_MAX + 1\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"rate: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let rate_value = &parsed_value[\"rate\"];\n\n            if rate_value.is_string() {\n                // String representation - cannot convert to float\n                let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n                assert!(\n                    result.is_none(),\n                    \"Overflow string '{}' should not convert to float ({})\",\n                    input,\n                    description\n                );\n\n                // Verify type mismatch error handling\n                let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"overflow\");\n                assert!(\n                    error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for float overflow {}\",\n                    description\n                );\n            } else if rate_value.is_f64() {\n                let float_value = rate_value.as_f64().unwrap();\n                // Check if it's infinity (overflow indicator)\n                assert!(\n                    float_value.is_infinite() || float_value <= f64::MAX,\n                    \"Float overflow should result in infinity or be clamped for {}\",\n                    description\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Verify error handling for float overflow scenario\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"overflow\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for float overflow {}\",\n            description\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_floating_point_underflow_values() {\n    /// Test: Floating point underflow scenarios\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that values below f64::MIN (most negative) are\n    /// properly handled, as well as subnormal numbers.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | -1e400 | Negative value below f64::MIN |\n    /// | -1e1000 | Extremely negative value |\n    /// | -1.7976931348623157e+309 | f64::MIN - 1 |\n    /// | 1e-400 | Extremely small subnormal |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"-1e400\", \"negative exponential 400\"),\n        (\"-1e1000\", \"negative exponential 1000\"),\n        (\"-1.7976931348623157e+309\", \"f64_MIN - 1\"),\n        (\"1e-400\", \"extremely small subnormal\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"rate: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let rate_value = &parsed_value[\"rate\"];\n\n            if rate_value.is_string() {\n                // String representation - cannot convert to float\n                let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n                assert!(\n                    result.is_none(),\n                    \"Underflow string '{}' should not convert to float ({})\",\n                    input,\n                    description\n                );\n\n                // Verify type mismatch error handling\n                let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"underflow\");\n                assert!(\n                    error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                    \"Type mismatch error should be created for float underflow {}\",\n                    description\n                );\n            } else if rate_value.is_f64() {\n                let float_value = rate_value.as_f64().unwrap();\n                // Check if it's negative infinity (underflow indicator)\n                assert!(float_value.is_infinite() || float_value >= f64::MIN || float_value == 0.0,\n                    \"Float underflow should result in -infinity, be clamped, or underflow to 0 for {}\", description);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Verify error handling for float underflow scenario\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"underflow\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for float underflow {}\",\n            description\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_floating_point_precision_limits() {\n    /// Test: Floating point precision boundary cases\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify behavior at precision limits and rounding boundaries.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | 1.7976931348623157e+308 | f64::MAX (largest finite) |\n    /// | -1.7976931348623157e+308 | f64::MIN (most negative finite) |\n    /// | 2.2250738585072014e-308 | f64::MIN_SUBNORMAL |\n    /// | 4.940656458412465e-324 | f64::MIN_POSITIVE |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"1.7976931348623157e+308\", \"f64_MAX\"),\n        (\"-1.7976931348623157e+308\", \"f64_MIN\"),\n        (\"2.2250738585072014e-308\", \"min_subnormal\"),\n        (\"4.940656458412465e-324\", \"min_positive\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"rate: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let rate_value = &value[\"rate\"];\n\n        // Verify conversion to float succeeds or handles edge case gracefully\n        let result = rate_value.as_f64();\n\n        if let Some(float_value) = result {\n            // Verify the value is representable\n            if description == \"f64_MAX\" {\n                assert!(\n                    float_value <= f64::MAX,\n                    \"f64_MAX value should be <= f64::MAX\"\n                );\n            } else if description == \"f64_MIN\" {\n                assert!(\n                    float_value >= f64::MIN,\n                    \"f64_MIN value should be >= f64::MIN\"\n                );\n            } else if description == \"min_positive\" {\n                assert!(\n                    float_value >= 0.0 && float_value <= f64::MIN_POSITIVE,\n                    \"min_positive value should be in valid range\"\n                );\n            }\n        } else {\n            // If conversion fails, verify error handling\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"rate\", \"float\", \"precision_limit\");\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for precision limit {}\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_denormal_and_nan_float_values() {\n    /// Test: Special floating point values\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify handling of NaN, infinity, and denormal numbers.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | .nan | NaN value |\n    /// | .inf | Positive infinity |\n    /// | -.inf | Negative infinity |\n    /// | 0.0 | Zero (can be positive or negative) |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (r#\"rate: .nan\"#, \"rate\", \"nan\"),\n        (r#\"timeout: .inf\"#, \"timeout\", \"positive_infinity\"),\n        (r#\"ratio: -.inf\"#, \"ratio\", \"negative_infinity\"),\n        (r#\"value: 0.0\"#, \"value\", \"zero\"),\n        (r#\"limit: -0.0\"#, \"limit\", \"negative_zero\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (yaml, field_name, description) in test_cases {\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[field_name];\n\n        let result = field_value.as_f64();\n\n        if let Some(float_value) = result {\n            match description {\n                \"nan\" => {\n                    assert!(\n                        float_value.is_nan(),\n                        \"Value should be NaN for {}\",\n                        description\n                    );\n                }\n                \"positive_infinity\" => {\n                    assert!(\n                        float_value.is_infinite() && float_value > 0.0,\n                        \"Value should be positive infinity for {}\",\n                        description\n                    );\n                }\n                \"negative_infinity\" => {\n                    assert!(\n                        float_value.is_infinite() && float_value < 0.0,\n                        \"Value should be negative infinity for {}\",\n                        description\n                    );\n                }\n                \"zero\" | \"negative_zero\" => {\n                    assert!(\n                        float_value == 0.0,\n                        \"Value should be zero for {}\",\n                        description\n                    );\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        } else {\n            // If conversion fails, verify error handling\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(field_name, \"float\", description);\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} special float value\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Range Limit Violation Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_u16_range_limit_violations() {\n    /// Test: Values exceeding u16 range (0 to 65535)\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding the u16 range are properly\n    /// rejected when a u16 is expected (common for port numbers, etc.).\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | 65536 | u16::MAX + 1 |\n    /// | 70000 | Value above u16::MAX |\n    /// | 100000 | Large value above u16 |\n    /// | -1 | Negative value (below 0) |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"65536\", \"u16_MAX + 1\"),\n        (\"70000\", \"70000\"),\n        (\"100000\", \"100000\"),\n        (\"99999\", \"99999\"),\n        (\"-1\", \"negative\"),\n        (\"-100\", \"negative_100\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"port: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let port_value = &parsed_value[\"port\"];\n\n            // For negative values, verify it's negative\n            if input.starts_with('-') {\n                if port_value.is_i64() {\n                    let int_value = port_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n                    assert!(\n                        int_value < 0,\n                        \"Value should be negative for {} ({})\",\n                        input,\n                        description\n                    );\n\n                    // Verify type mismatch error for u16 range violation\n                    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"u16\", \"negative\");\n                    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                        \"Type mismatch error should be created for negative value in u16 context ({})\",\n                        description);\n                }\n            } else {\n                // For values above u16::MAX\n                if port_value.is_i64() {\n                    let int_value = port_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n                    if int_value > u16::MAX as i64 {\n                        // Verify type mismatch error for u16 range violation\n                        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"u16\", \"overflow\");\n                        assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                            \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow in u16 context ({})\",\n                            description);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_u8_range_limit_violations() {\n    /// Test: Values exceeding u8 range (0 to 255)\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding the u8 range are properly\n    /// rejected when a u8 is expected.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | 256 | u8::MAX + 1 |\n    /// | 1000 | Value above u8::MAX |\n    /// | 500 | Value above u8::MAX |\n    /// | -1 | Negative value |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"256\", \"u8_MAX + 1\"),\n        (\"1000\", \"1000\"),\n        (\"500\", \"500\"),\n        (\"300\", \"300\"),\n        (\"-1\", \"negative\"),\n        (\"-10\", \"negative_10\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"flags: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let flags_value = &parsed_value[\"flags\"];\n\n            if input.starts_with('-') {\n                if flags_value.is_i64() {\n                    let int_value = flags_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n                    assert!(\n                        int_value < 0,\n                        \"Value should be negative for {} ({})\",\n                        input,\n                        description\n                    );\n\n                    // Verify type mismatch error for u8 range violation\n                    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"flags\", \"u8\", \"negative\");\n                    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                        \"Type mismatch error should be created for negative value in u8 context ({})\",\n                        description);\n                }\n            } else {\n                if flags_value.is_i64() {\n                    let int_value = flags_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n                    if int_value > u8::MAX as i64 {\n                        // Verify type mismatch error for u8 range violation\n                        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"flags\", \"u8\", \"overflow\");\n                        assert!(\n                            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                            \"Type mismatch error should be created for overflow in u8 context ({})\",\n                            description\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_i32_range_limit_violations() {\n    /// Test: Values exceeding i32 range\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify that values exceeding the i32 range are properly\n    /// rejected when an i32 is expected.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | 2147483648 | i32::MAX + 1 |\n    /// | -2147483649 | i32::MIN - 1 |\n    /// | 5000000000 | Large positive value |\n    /// | -5000000000 | Large negative value |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"2147483648\", \"i32_MAX + 1\"),\n        (\"-2147483649\", \"i32_MIN - 1\"),\n        (\"5000000000\", \"large_positive\"),\n        (\"-5000000000\", \"large_negative\"),\n        (\"10000000000\", \"very_large_positive\"),\n        (\"-10000000000\", \"very_large_negative\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"count: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let count_value = &parsed_value[\"count\"];\n\n            if count_value.is_i64() {\n                let int_value = count_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n\n                // Verify value exceeds i32 range\n                let exceeds_range = if int_value > 0 {\n                    int_value > i32::MAX as i64\n                } else {\n                    int_value < i32::MIN as i64\n                };\n\n                if exceeds_range {\n                    // Verify type mismatch error for i32 range violation\n                    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\n                        \"count\",\n                        \"i32\",\n                        if int_value > 0 {\n                            \"overflow\"\n                        } else {\n                            \"underflow\"\n                        },\n                    );\n                    assert!(\n                        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                        \"Type mismatch error should be created for {} in i32 context ({})\",\n                        if int_value > 0 {\n                            \"overflow\"\n                        } else {\n                            \"underflow\"\n                        },\n                        description\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_range_boundary_values() {\n    /// Test: Boundary values at type limits\n    ///\n    /// These test cases verify exact boundary values and values just beyond boundaries.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Type | Boundary Input | Description | Valid |\n    /// |------|----------------|-------------|-------|\n    /// | u8 | 255 | u8::MAX (valid) | true |\n    /// | u8 | 256 | u8::MAX + 1 (invalid) | false |\n    /// | i8 | 127 | i8::MAX (valid) | true |\n    /// | i8 | 128 | i8::MAX + 1 (invalid) | false |\n    /// | i8 | -128 | i8::MIN (valid) | true |\n    /// | i8 | -129 | i8::MIN - 1 (invalid) | false |\n    /// | u16 | 65535 | u16::MAX (valid) | true |\n    /// | u16 | 65536 | u16::MAX + 1 (invalid) | false |\n    /// | i16 | 32767 | i16::MAX (valid) | true |\n    /// | i16 | 32768 | i16::MAX + 1 (invalid) | false |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (\"255\", \"u8\", \"u8_MAX\", true),\n        (\"256\", \"u8\", \"u8_MAX + 1\", false),\n        (\"127\", \"i8\", \"i8_MAX\", true),\n        (\"128\", \"i8\", \"i8_MAX + 1\", false),\n        (\"-128\", \"i8\", \"i8_MIN\", true),\n        (\"-129\", \"i8\", \"i8_MIN - 1\", false),\n        (\"65535\", \"u16\", \"u16_MAX\", true),\n        (\"65536\", \"u16\", \"u16_MAX + 1\", false),\n        (\"32767\", \"i16\", \"i16_MAX\", true),\n        (\"32768\", \"i16\", \"i16_MAX + 1\", false),\n        (\"-32768\", \"i16\", \"i16_MIN\", true),\n        (\"-32769\", \"i16\", \"i16_MIN - 1\", false),\n    ];\n\n    for (input, target_type, description, should_be_valid) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", input);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        if let Ok(parsed_value) = value {\n            let value_field = &parsed_value[\"value\"];\n\n            if value_field.is_i64() {\n                let int_value = value_field.as_i64().unwrap();\n\n                // Check if value is within range for target type\n                let is_in_range = match target_type {\n                    \"u8\" => int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u8::MAX as i64,\n                    \"i8\" => int_value >= i8::MIN as i64 && int_value <= i8::MAX as i64,\n                    \"u16\" => int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u16::MAX as i64,\n                    \"i16\" => int_value >= i16::MIN as i64 && int_value <= i16::MAX as i64,\n                    _ => true,\n                };\n\n                assert_eq!(\n                    is_in_range,\n                    should_be_valid,\n                    \"Value {} should {}be in range for {} ({})\",\n                    input,\n                    if should_be_valid { \"\" } else { \"not \" },\n                    target_type,\n                    description\n                );\n\n                // If out of range, verify error handling\n                if !is_in_range {\n                    let error_type = if int_value < 0 {\n                        \"underflow\"\n                    } else {\n                        \"overflow\"\n                    };\n                    let error = {"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260713214027e5842cdf41d44a2d","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-4.7","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_8747f59fe3e446abbb2c4086","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/ARMOR/tests/int32_to_uint32_boundary_test.rs","content":"//! Int32 to UInt32 Boundary Condition Tests\n//!\n//! Comprehensive edge case and boundary condition tests for int32 to uint32\n//! negative conversion. These tests ensure that all boundary values are properly\n//! handled and produce appropriate errors when negative int32 values cannot be\n//! converted to uint32.\n//!\n//! # Test Categories\n//!\n//! 1. **Int32 Minimum Value Tests** - Test i32::MIN (-2147483648) edge cases\n//! 2. **Maximum Negative Values** - Test values closest to zero (-1, -2, etc.)\n//! 3. **Zero Boundary Cases** - Test the transition between negative and positive\n//! 4. **Range Tests** - Test various magnitudes of negative values\n//! 5. **Special Boundary Values** - Powers of 2, bit boundaries, etc.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\nuse serde_yaml::Value;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Int32 Minimum Value Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_value() {\n    /// Test: int32::MIN (-2147483648) cannot convert to uint32\n    ///\n    /// This is the absolute minimum value for a signed 32-bit integer.\n    /// In two's complement representation, this is 0x80000000.\n    ///\n    /// # Why This Matters\n    ///\n    /// - int32::MIN is the most negative value representable in int32\n    /// - It has a special binary representation (only the sign bit set)\n    /// - Converting to uint32 would be an overflow/underflow condition\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -2147483648\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for int32::MIN\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's parsed as a negative integer\n    assert!(\n        field_value.is_i64(),\n        \"int32::MIN should be parsed as i64\"\n    );\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -2147483648, \"Value should be int32::MIN\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"int32::MIN is negative\");\n\n    // Verify it would not fit in uint32 range\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"int32::MIN should not fit in uint32 range\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_min\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for int32::MIN\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned type\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ int32::MIN (-2147483648) properly rejected for uint32 conversion\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_plus_variations() {\n    /// Test: Values near int32::MIN\n    ///\n    /// Test values just above int32::MIN to ensure boundary detection works.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description |\n    /// |-------|-------------|\n    /// | -2147483648 | int32::MIN (exact) |\n    /// | -2147483647 | int32::MIN + 1 |\n    /// | -2147483640 | int32::MIN + 8 |\n    /// | -2147483638 | int32::MIN + 10 |\n    /// | -2147483500 | int32::MIN + 148 |\n    /// | -2147483000 | int32::MIN + 648 |\n    /// | -2147480000 | int32::MIN + 3648 |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-2147483648i64, \"int32::MIN exact\"),\n        (-2147483647i64, \"int32::MIN + 1\"),\n        (-2147483640i64, \"int32::MIN + 8\"),\n        (-2147483638i64, \"int32::MIN + 10\"),\n        (-2147483500i64, \"int32::MIN + 148\"),\n        (-2147483000i64, \"int32::MIN + 648\"),\n        (-2147480000i64, \"int32::MIN + 3648\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            int_value, expected_value,\n            \"Value should match expected for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative for {}\", description);\n\n        // Verify it would not fit in uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Value {} should not fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            expected_value, description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\"✓ {} ({}): properly rejected\", expected_value, description);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All int32::MIN variations properly handled\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Maximum Negative Values (Closest to Zero)\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_maximum_negative_values() {\n    /// Test: Maximum negative values (closest to zero)\n    ///\n    /// These are the \"largest\" negative integers in the mathematical sense\n    /// (closest to zero, but still negative).\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Description | Distance from Zero |\n    /// |-------|-------------|-------------------|\n    /// | -1 | Largest negative value | 1 |\n    /// | -2 | Second largest negative | 2 |\n    /// | -3 | Third largest negative | 3 |\n    /// | -10 | Small negative magnitude | 10 |\n    /// | -100 | Century boundary | 100 |\n    /// | -128 | Power of 2 boundary | 128 |\n    /// | -255 | Byte boundary - 1 | 255 |\n    /// | -256 | Byte boundary | 256 |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"negative one (closest to zero)\"),\n        (-2i64, \"negative two\"),\n        (-3i64, \"negative three\"),\n        (-5i64, \"negative five\"),\n        (-10i64, \"negative ten\"),\n        (-15i64, \"negative fifteen\"),\n        (-16i64, \"negative sixteen (2^4)\"),\n        (-31i64, \"negative thirty-one\"),\n        (-32i64, \"negative thirty-two (2^5)\"),\n        (-63i64, \"negative sixty-three\"),\n        (-64i64, \"negative sixty-four (2^6)\"),\n        (-100i64, \"negative hundred\"),\n        (-127i64, \"negative 127 (2^7 - 1)\"),\n        (-128i64, \"negative 128 (2^7)\"),\n        (-255i64, \"negative 255 (2^8 - 1)\"),\n        (-256i64, \"negative 256 (2^8)\"),\n        (-512i64, \"negative 512 (2^9)\"),\n        (-1000i64, \"negative thousand\"),\n        (-1024i64, \"negative 1024 (2^10)\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            int_value, expected_value,\n            \"Value should match expected for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative for {}\", description);\n\n        // All negative values, even those close to zero, cannot convert to uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Negative value {} should not fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            expected_value, description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(\n            error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n            \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\"✓ {} ({}): properly rejected as negative\", expected_value, description);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All maximum negative values (closest to zero) properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_one_special_case() {\n    /// Test: -1 as a special boundary case\n    ///\n    /// -1 is particularly important because:\n    /// 1. It's the closest negative integer to zero\n    /// 2. In two's complement, it's all 1s (0xFFFFFFFF for 32-bit)\n    /// 3. When incorrectly cast to unsigned, it becomes UINT32_MAX\n    /// 4. This is a very common bug in C/C++ code\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -1\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for -1\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's -1\n    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"-1 should be parsed as i64\");\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -1, \"Value should be exactly -1\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"-1 is negative\");\n\n    // Important: -1 does NOT fit in uint32\n    // (Even though some buggy code might incorrectly cast it)\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"-1 should not be considered valid for uint32\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"negative_one\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for -1\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"-1\"),\n        \"Error should indicate negative value\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ -1 properly rejected (critical boundary case)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Zero Boundary Cases\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_boundary_transition() {\n    /// Test: Zero boundary and transition from negative to non-negative\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies the behavior at the exact boundary between\n    /// negative and non-negative values.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Sign | Can be uint32? | Description |\n    /// |-------|------|----------------|-------------|\n    /// | -1 | Negative | NO | Last negative value |\n    /// | 0 | Zero | YES | First non-negative value |\n    /// | 1 | Positive | YES | First positive value |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"negative\", false, \"last negative before zero\"),\n        (0i64, \"zero\", true, \"exact zero (boundary)\"),\n        (1i64, \"positive\", true, \"first positive after zero\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (value, sign_description, should_fit_uint32, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", value);\n        let parsed: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            parsed.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let parsed = parsed.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &parsed[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's parsed as integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(int_value, value, \"Value should match expected\");\n\n        // Check if fits in uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert_eq!(\n            fits_in_uint32, should_fit_uint32,\n            \"Value {} should {}fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            value,\n            if should_fit_uint32 { \"\" } else { \"not \" },\n            description\n        );\n\n        // For negative values, verify error handling\n        if !should_fit_uint32 {\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n\n            let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n            assert!(\n                error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n                \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n\n        println!(\"✓ {}: {} sign, uint32 fit={}\", value, sign_description, fits_in_uint32);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ Zero boundary transition properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_with_negative_context() {\n    /// Test: Zero in context where negative was expected\n    ///\n    /// This tests the edge case where a field might conceptually be\n    /// related to negative values, but zero is technically valid.\n    let yaml = r#\"value: 0\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for 0\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's zero\n    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"0 should be parsed as i64\");\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, 0, \"Value should be exactly 0\");\n\n    // Zero IS valid for uint32 (it's in the range [0, UINT32_MAX])\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        fits_in_uint32,\n        \"Zero should fit in uint32 range\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify no type mismatch error for zero (it's valid!)\n    // We only test error creation for actual negative values\n\n    println!(\"✓ Zero is valid for uint32 (boundary case handled correctly)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Range Tests - Various Negative Magnitudes\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_values_by_magnitude_range() {\n    /// Test: Negative values across different magnitude ///\n    /// 1. **Small magnitude** (1-100): -1, -10, -50, -100\n    /// 2. **Medium magnitude** (100-10,000): -500, -1000, -5000, -10000\n    /// 3. **Large magnitude** (10,000-1,000,000): -50000, -100000, -500000, -1000000\n    /// 4. **Very large magnitude** (1M-100M): -5M, -10M, -50M, -100M\n    /// 5. **Extreme magnitude** (100M-int32::MAX): -500M, -1B, -int32::MAX\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        // Small magnitude (1-100)\n        (-1i64, \"magnitude 1\"),\n        (-5i64, \"magnitude 5\"),\n        (-10i64, \"magnitude 10\"),\n        (-15i64, \"magnitude 15\"),\n        (-25i64, \"magnitude 25\"),\n        (-50i64, \"magnitude 50\"),\n        (-75i64, \"magnitude 75\"),\n        (-100i64, \"magnitude 100\"),\n        // Medium magnitude (100-10,000)\n        (-500i64, \"magnitude 500\"),\n        (-1000i64, \"magnitude 1,000\"),\n        (-2500i64, \"magnitude 2,500\"),\n        (-5000i64, \"magnitude 5,000\"),\n        (-7500i64, \"magnitude 7,500\"),\n        (-10000i64, \"magnitude 10,000\"),\n        // Large magnitude (10,000-1,000,000)\n        (-25000i64, \"magnitude 25,000\"),\n        (-50000i64, \"magnitude 50,000\"),\n        (-75000i64, \"magnitude 75,000\"),\n        (-100000i64, \"magnitude 100,000\"),\n        (-250000i64, \"magnitude 250,000\"),\n        (-500000i64, \"magnitude 500,000\"),\n        (-750000i64, \"magnitude 750,000\"),\n        (-1000000i64, \"magnitude 1,000,000\"),\n        // Very large magnitude (1M-100M)\n        (-5000000i64, \"magnitude 5,000,000\"),\n        (-10000000i64, \"magnitude 10,000,000\"),\n        (-25000000i64, \"magnitude 25,000,000\"),\n        (-50000000i64, \"magnitude 50,000,000\"),\n        (-75000000i64, \"magnitude 75,000,000\"),\n        (-100000000i64, \"magnitude 100,000,000\"),\n        // Extreme magnitude (100M to int32::MIN)\n        (-250000000i64, \"magnitude 250,000,000\"),\n        (-500000000i64, \"magnitude 500,000,000\"),\n        (-750000000i64, \"magnitude 750,000,000\"),\n        (-1000000000i64, \"magnitude 1,000,000,000\"),\n        (-1500000000i64, \"magnitude 1,500,000,000\"),\n        (-2000000000i64, \"magnitude 2,000,000,000\"),\n        (-2147483647i64, \"magnitude 2,147,483,647 (int32::MAX negative)\"),\n        (-2147483648i64, \"magnitude 2,147,483,648 (int32::MIN)\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, magnitude_description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            magnitude_description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            magnitude_description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            int_value, expected_value,\n            \"Value should match expected for {}\",\n            magnitude_description\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // All negative values cannot convert to uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Negative value {} should not fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            expected_value, magnitude_description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n expected_value, magnitude_description\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All negative magnitude ranges properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_values_by_power_of_two_boundaries() {\n \"-2^4\", \"negative 16 (2^4)\"),\n        (-32i64, \"-2^5\", \"negative 32 (2^5)\"),\n        (-64i64, \"-2^6\", \"negative 64 (2^6)\"),\n        (-128i64, \"-2^7\", \"negative 128 (2^7)\"),\n \"-2^30\", \"negative 1073741824 (2^30)\"),\n        (-2147483648i64, \"-2^31\", \"negative 2147483648 (2^31, int32::MIN)\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, power_of_two, description) in  \"YAML parsing should succeed for {} ({})\",\n            power_of_two, description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\n            \"✓ {} ({}): properly rejected\",\n            expected_value, power_of_two\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All power-of-2 boundary values properly handled\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Special Boundary Values\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_common_negative_constants() {\n    /// Test: Common negative constants that might appear in configurations\n    ///\n    /// These are negative values that commonly appear in real-world\n    /// configuration files and should be properly rejected for uint32.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Common Usage |\n    /// |-------|--------------|\n    /// | -1 | Error codes, \"not set\" values |\n    /// | -10 | Error codes, small penalties |\n    /// | -100 | Timeout defaults, penalties |\n    /// | -1000 | Millisecond offsets |\n    /// | -3600 | One hour in seconds |\n    /// | -86400 | One day in seconds |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"error_code_or_not_set\"),\n        (-10i64, \"small_error_code_or_penalty\"),\n        (-100i64, \"default_timeout_or_penalty\"),\n        (-1000i64, \"millisecond_offset\"),\n        (-3600i64, \"one_hour_seconds_negative\"),\n        (-86400i64, \"one_day_seconds_negative\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, usage_context) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            int_value, expected_value,\n            \"Value should match expected for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n        assert!(\n            error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n            \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        println!(\"✓ {} ({}): properly rejected\", expected_value, usage_context);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All common negative constants properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_values_near_uint32_boundaries() {\n    /// Test: Negative values that would overflow uint32 boundaries\n    ///\n    /// These tests verify that negative values are properly rejected\n    /// even when their absolute value might be within uint32 range.\n    ///\n    /// The key insight: For uint32, the valid range is [0, 4294967295].\n    /// ANY negative value is invalid, regardless of its magnitude.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Absolute Value | Would fit as uint32 | Should Reject? |\n    /// |-------|----------------|-------------------|----------------|\n    /// | -1 | 1 | Yes (as 1) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -100 | 100 | Yes (as 100) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000 | 1000 | Yes (as 1000) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000 | 1000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000000 | 1000000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, 1u64, \"absolute value 1\"),\n        (-100i64, 100u64, \"absolute value 100\"),\n        (-1000i64, 1000u64, \"absolute value 1000\"),\n        (-10000i64, 10000u64, \"absolute value 10000\"),\n        (-100000i64, 100000u64, \"absolute value 100000\"),\n        (-1000000i64, 1000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000\"),\n        (-10000000i64, 10000000u64, \"absolute value 10000000\"),\n        (-100000000i64, 100000000u64, \"absolute value 100000000\"),\n        (-1000000000i64, 1000000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000000\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (negative_value, absolute_value, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", negative_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(int_value, negative_value, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // The absolute value WOULD fit in uint32...\n        assert!(\n            absolute_value <= u32::MAX as u64,\n            \"Absolute value should fit in uint32 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        // ...but the negative value itself must NOT be accepted\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Negative value {} should NOT fit in uint32 ({}) - sign matters!\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\n            \"✓ {} ({}): negative sign correctly prevents conversion\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All negative values properly rejected (sign check works correctly)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Summary and Coverage Verification\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_to_uint32_boundary_coverage_summary() {\n    /// Test: Verify comprehensive coverage of all boundary conditions\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies that we have covered all the critical boundary\n    /// conditions for int32 to uint32 negative conversion.\n    ///\n    /// # Coverage Checklist\n    ///\n    /// - [x] int32::MIN (-2147483648) - absolute minimum\n    /// - [x] Maximum negative values (-1, -2, -3, etc.) - closest to zero\n    /// - [x] Zero boundary case (transition let is_negative = value < 0;\n        let fits_uint32 = value >= 0 && value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        let should_reject = is_negative;\n\n        println!(\n            \"{}: {} -> negative={}, Zero (0) is valid - it's the boundary between valid/invalid\");\n    println!(\"3. int32::MIN (-2147483648) is the extreme edge case\");\n    println!(\"4. -1 is the 'closest' negative to zero, but still invalid\");\n    println!(\"5. Int32 to UInt32 Boundary Condition Tests\n//!\n//! Comprehensive edge case and boundary condition tests for int32 to uint32\n//! negative conversion. These tests ensure that all boundary values are properly\n//! handled and produce appropriate errors when negative int32 values cannot be\n//! converted to uint32.\n//!\n//! # Test Categories\n//!\n//! 1. **Int32 Minimum Value Tests** - Test i32::MIN (-2147483648) edge cases\n//! 2. **Maximum Negative Values** - Test values closest to zero (-1, -2, etc.)\n//! 3. **Zero Boundary Cases** - Test the transition between negative and positive\n//! 4. **Range Tests** - Test various magnitudes of negative values\n//! 5. **Special Boundary Values** - Powers of 2, bit boundaries, etc.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::ParseError;\nuse serde_yaml::Value;\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Int32 Minimum Value Tests\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_value() {\n    /// Test: int32::MIN (-2147483648) cannot convert to uint32\n    ///\n    /// This is the absolute minimum value for a signed 32-bit integer.\n    /// In two's complement representation, this is 0x80000000.\n    ///\n    /// # Why This Matters\n    ///\n    /// - int32::MIN is the most negative value representable in int32\n    /// - It has a special binary representation (only the sign bit set)\n    /// - Converting to uint32 would be an overflow/underflow condition\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -2147483648\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for int32::MIN\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's parsed as a negative integer\n    assert!(\n        field_value.is_i64(),\n        \"int32::MIN should be parsed as i64\"\n    );\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -2147483648, \"Value should be int32::MIN\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"int32::MIN is negative\");\n\n    // Verify it would not fit in uint32 range\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"int32::MIN should not fit in uint32 range\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error is properly created\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_min\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for int32::MIN\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned type\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ int32::MIN (-2147483648) properly rejected for uint32 conversion\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_minimum_plus_variations() {\n    /// Test: Values near int32::MIN\n    ///\n    /// Test values just above int32::MIN to ensure boundary detection particularly important because:\n    /// 1. It's the closest negative integer to zero\n    /// 2. In two's complement, it's all 1s (0xFFFFFFFF for 32-bit)\n    /// 3. When incorrectly cast to unsigned, it becomes UINT32_MAX\n    /// 4. This is a very common bug in C/C++ code\n    let yaml = r#\"value: -1\"#;\n    let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml);\n\n    assert!(value.is_ok(), \"YAML parsing should succeed for -1\");\n\n    let value = value.unwrap();\n    let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n    // Verify it's -1\n    assert!(field_value.is_i64(), \"-1 should be parsed as i64\");\n    let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(int_value, -1, \"Value should be exactly -1\");\n    assert!(int_value < 0, \"-1 is negative\");\n\n    // Important: -1 does NOT fit in uint32\n    // (Even though some buggy code might incorrectly cast it)\n    let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n    assert!(\n        !fits_in_uint32,\n        \"-1 should not be considered valid for uint32\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify type mismatch error\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"negative_one\");\n    assert!(\n        error.is_type_mismatch(),\n        \"Type mismatch error should be created for -1\"\n    );\n\n    let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n        \"Error should mention uint32/unsigned\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        error_msg.contains(\"negative\") || error_msg.contains(\"-1\"),\n        \"Error should indicate negative value\"\n    );\n\n    println!(\"✓ -1 properly rejected (critical boundary case)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Zero Boundary Cases\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_boundary_transition() {\n    /// Test: Zero boundary and transition from negative to non-negative\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies the behavior at the exact boundary between\n    /// negative and non-negative values.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Sign | Can be uint32? | Description |\n    /// |-------|------|----------------|-------------|\n    /// | -1 | Negative | NO | Last negative value |\n    /// | 0 | Zero | YES | First true, \"exact zero (boundary)\"),\n        (1i64, \"positive\", true, \"first positive after zero\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (value, sign_description, should_fit_uint32, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", value);\n        let parsed: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            parsed.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let parsed = parsed.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &parsed[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's parsed as integer\n assert_eq!(int_value, value, \"Value should match expected\");\n\n        // Check if fits in uint32\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert_eq!(\n            fits_in_uint32, should_fit_uint32,\n            \"Value {} should {}fit in uint32 ({})\",\n            value,\n            if should_fit_uint32 { \"\" } else { \"not \" },\n            description\n        );\n\n        // For negative values, verify error handling\n        if !should_fit_uint32 {\n            let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n            assert!(\n                error.is_type_mismatch(),\n                \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n\n            let error_msg = format!(\"{}\", error.kind);\n            assert!(\n                error_msg.contains(\"uint32\") || error_msg.contains(\"unsigned\"),\n                \"Error should mention uint32 for {}\",\n                description\n            );\n        }\n\n        println!(\"✓ {}: {} sign, uint32 fit={}\", value, sign_description, fits_in_uint32);\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ Zero boundary transition properly handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_zero_with_negative_context() {\n    /// Test: Zero in context where negative was expected\n    ///\n    /// This tests   /// Test: Negative values at power of 2 boundaries\n    ///\n    /// These test values at powers of 2 and adjacent values to test\n    /// boundary detection around bit-width transitions.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// Powers of 2 and their negatives:\n    /// - 2^0 = 1, test -1\n    /// - 2^1 = 2, test -2\n    /// - 2^2 = 4, test -4\n    /// - ...\n    /// - 2^16 = 65536, test \"-2^30\", \"negative 1073741824 (2^30)\"),\n        (-2147483648i64, \"-2^31\", \"negative 2147483648 (2^31, int32::MIN)\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, power_of_two, description) in test_common_negative_constants() {\n    /// Test: Common negative constants that might appear in configurations\n    ///\n    /// These are negative values that commonly appear in real-world\n    /// configuration files and should be properly rejected for uint32.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Common Usage |\n    /// |-------|--------------|\n    /// | -1 | Error codes, \"not set\" values |\n    /// | -10 | Error codes, small penalties |\n    /// | -100 | Timeout defaults, penalties |\n    /// | -1000 | Millisecond offsets |\n    /// | -3600 | One hour in seconds |\n    /// | -86400 | One day in seconds |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, \"error_code_or_not_set\"),\n        (-10i64, \"small_error_code_or_penalty\"),\n        (-100i64, \"default_timeout_or_penalty\"),\n        (-1000i64, \"millisecond_offset\"),\n        (-3600i64, \"one_hour_seconds_negative\"),\n        (-86400i64, \"one_day_seconds_negative\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (expected_value, usage_context) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", expected_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            usage_context\n usage_context\n        );\n        assert!(int_value < 0, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            usage_context\n        );\n\n        let handled\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_negative_values_near_uint32_boundaries() {\n    /// Test: Negative values that would overflow uint32 boundaries\n    ///\n    /// These tests verify that negative values are properly rejected\n    /// even when their absolute value might be within uint32 range.\n    ///\n    /// The key insight: For uint32, the valid range is [0, 4294967295].\n    /// ANY negative value is invalid, regardless of its magnitude.\n    ///\n    /// # Test Cases\n    ///\n    /// | Input | Absolute Value | Would fit as uint32 | Should Reject? |\n    /// |-------|----------------|-------------------|----------------|\n    /// | -1 | 1 | Yes (as 1) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -100 | 100 | Yes (as 100) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000 | 1000 | Yes (as 1000) | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000 | 1000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    /// | -1000000000 | 1000000000 | Yes | YES (it's negative!) |\n    let test_cases = vec![\n        (-1i64, 1u64, \"absolute value 1\"),\n        (-100i64, 100u64, \"absolute value 100\"),\n        (-1000i64, 1000u64, \"absolute value 1000\"),\n        (-10000i64, 10000u64, \"absolute value 10000\"),\n        (-100000i64, 100000u64, \"absolute value 100000\"),\n        (-1000000i64, 1000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000\"),\n        (-10000000i64, 10000000u64, \"absolute value 10000000\"),\n        (-100000000i64, 100000000u64, \"absolute value 100000000\"),\n        (-1000000000i64, 1000000000u64, \"absolute value 1000000000\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (negative_value, absolute_value, description) in test_cases {\n        let yaml = format!(\"value: {}\", negative_value);\n        let value: Result<Value, _> = serde_yaml::from_str(&yaml);\n\n        assert!(\n            value.is_ok(),\n            \"YAML parsing should succeed for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        let value = value.unwrap();\n        let field_value = &value[\"value\"];\n\n        // Verify it's a negative integer\n        assert!(\n            field_value.is_i64(),\n            \"Value should be i64 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n        let int_value = field_value.as_i64().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(int_value, negative_value, \"Value should be negative\");\n\n        // The absolute value WOULD fit in uint32...\n        assert!(\n            absolute_value <= u32::MAX as u64,\n            \"Absolute value should fit in uint32 for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        // ...but the negative value itself must NOT be accepted\n        let fits_in_uint32 = int_value >= 0 && int_value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        assert!(\n            !fits_in_uint32,\n            \"Negative value {} should NOT fit in uint32 ({}) - sign matters!\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n\n        // Verify type mismatch error\n        let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"value\", \"uint32\", \"int32_negative\");\n        assert!(\n            error.is_type_mismatch(),\n            \"Type mismatch error should be created for {}\",\n            description\n        );\n\n        println!(\n            \"✓ {} ({}): negative sign correctly prevents conversion\",\n            negative_value, description\n        );\n    }\n\n    println!(\"✓ All negative values properly rejected (sign check works correctly)\");\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Summary and Coverage Verification\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_int32_to_uint32_boundary_coverage_summary() {\n    /// Test: Verify comprehensive coverage of all boundary conditions\n    ///\n    /// This test verifies that we have covered all the critical boundary\n    /// conditions for int32 to uint32 negative conversion.\n    ///\n    /// # Coverage Checklist\n    ///\n    /// - [x] int32::MIN (-2147483648) - absolute minimum\n    /// - [x] Maximum negative values (-1, -2, -3, etc.) - closest to zero\n    /// - [x] Zero boundary case (transition from -1 to 0 to 1)\n    /// - [x] Range tests across all magnitudes\n    /// - [x] Power-of-2 boundaries\n    /// - [x] Common negative constants\n    /// - [x] Values that would fit as unsigned but are negative\n    ///\n    /// This test documents the coverage and serves as a checklist for\n    /// future maintenance.\n\n    // Document key boundary values\n    let critical_values = vec![\n        (\"int32::MIN\", -2147483648i64),\n        (\"int32::MIN + 1\", -2147483647i64),\n        (\"near int32::MIN\", -2000000000i64),\n        (\"mid negative range\", -1000000000i64),\n        (\"large negative\", -1000000i64),\n        (\"medium negative\", -100000i64),\n        (\"small negative\", -1000i64),\n        (\"tiny negative\", -100i64),\n        (\"negative one\", -1i64),\n        (\"zero\", 0i64),\n        (\"positive one\", 1i64),\n    ];\n\n    println!(\"\\n=== Int32 to Uint32 Boundary Coverage Summary ===\\n\");\n\n    for (description, value) in critical_values {\n        let is_negative = value < 0;\n        let fits_uint32 = value >= 0 && value <= u32::MAX as i64;\n        let should_reject = is_negative;\n\n        println!(\n            \"{}: {} -> negative={}, fits_uint32={}, should_reject={}\",\n            description, value, is_negative, fits_uint32, should_reject\n        );\n\n        // Verify the logic is correct\n        if should_reject {\n            assert!(\n                !fits_uint32 || value < 0,\n                \"Rejected value should either not fit or be negative: {} ({})\",\n                value, description\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"\\n✓ All boundary conditions documented and verified\");\n    println!(\"✓ Coverage checklist complete\");\n    println!(\"\\nKey Takeaways:\");\n    println!(\"1. ALL negative int32 values are invalid for uint32 conversion\");\n    println!(\"2. Zero (0) is valid - it's the boundary between valid/invalid\");\n    println!(\"3. int32::MIN (-2147483648) is the extreme edge case\");\n    println!(\"4. -1 is the 'closest' negative to zero, but still invalid\");\n    println!(\"5. Magnitude doesn't matter - sign is the critical factor\");\n}\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
